substrates.substrate_A: various compounds including ortho-nitrobenzaldehyde, riboflavin, photoinitiators, spirobenzopyran, azobenzene

chemistry.product: various photoproducts including o-nitrosobenzoic acid, lumichrome, radical fragments, merocyanine forms
chemistry.bond_formed: various bond cleavages and formations depending on substrate

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: various excited states and radical intermediates

chemistry.scope_summary: Development of a methodology for determining photochemical quantum yields using LED irradiation and online UV-Vis monitoring. Demonstrated with multiple substrate classes including actinometers, photoinitiators, biological molecules, and molecular switches.

figures.key_information: Photoreactor setup with LED light source, spectrometer beam for inline monitoring, and magnetic stirring
figures.caption: Fig. 1 Schematic representation of the experimental setup for the online monitoring of photochemical reactions. The solution is irradiated with an LED (purple arrow) perpendicular to the UV-Vis spectrometer beam (black arrow). The sample is stirred to provide a homogeneous solution.

figures.mechanism_type: photochemical cleavage followed by intersystem crossing
figures.caption: Fig. 4 Irradiating 7.7  μ M ribo ﬂ avin ( 3 ) in 0.01 M phosphate bu ﬀ er with a 420 nm LED. Each spectrum = 10 s. The insert shows the absorbance decay at 446 nm under aerobic conditions, together with a mono-expo- nential  ﬁ t and the corresponding residuals used to determine the reac- tion rate and the quantum yield. | Scheme 1 Photoinduced  α -cleavage of initiators  4  and  5  to produce a phosphanoyl or germyl radical ( P •   or  G • ) and a benzoyl-type radical ( B •   or  B ' • ).

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Traditional photochemical quantum yield determination required time-consuming chemical actinometry methods
translation_logic.flow_advantage: This paper describes a batch method with online UV-Vis monitoring, not a flow system
translation_logic.what_changed: Introduction of LED irradiation with online UV-Vis spectroscopy monitoring
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Online monitoring reduces handling requirements compared to traditional actinometry

figure_descriptions: Figure 1 shows a photoreactor setup with LED light source and inline UV-Vis monitoring
Mechanistic scheme shows photochemical α-cleavage reactions

conflicts: Paper describes batch methodology for quantum yield determination, not batch-to-flow translation
notes: This paper describes a batch photochemical method for quantum yield determination using LED irradiation and online UV-Vis monitoring. It is not a batch-to-flow translation study, but rather a methodology development for improving traditional actinometry approaches.
